mnemonics.title: BINARY SEARCH RECURSION STEPS
mnemonics.content: LEARN IT: LowAndHighFindMid, CompareAndDecide, RecurseOrReturn - Remember: LO HI MID COMPARE RECURSE RETURN - each letter represents a step in the algorithm flow.

mnemonics.title: BASE CASE MEMORY
mnemonics.content: LOW > HIGH = GOODBYE (NOT FOUND) - This reminds you that when the lower index exceeds the upper index, the search is complete without finding the element.

mnemonics.title: TIME COMPLEXITY
mnemonics.content: LOG POWER: Remember that binary search gives LOGARITHMIC time complexity, making it POWERFUL for large datasets.

acronyms.expansion: Middle Index Determination - Calculated as low + (high - low) / 2 to prevent overflow
acronyms.usage: Used to divide the search space into two halves in each recursive call

acronyms.expansion: Target Compare Recurse - Three main operations in binary search: compare target with middle element and recursively search appropriate half
acronyms.usage: Remember the order of operations in each recursive call

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a number line where you're looking for a number. Each comparison cuts the remaining range in half, like folding a paper in half repeatedly until finding your mark.
Draw an inverted tree where the root is the full array, each level splits into two branches (left and right), and the depth represents the number of recursions needed - maximum depth is log₂(n).

analogies.analogy: Looking for a word in a dictionary. You don't read page by page; you open near the middle, see if your word comes before or after, then repeat on the appropriate half - this is exactly how binary search works.

analogies.concept: Finding a Person in a Building
analogies.analogy: Looking for someone in a 100-floor building by checking the middle floor, then going to the upper or lower half based on whether they're taller or shorter - halving your search each time.
